criteria_results.text: Product name 'AbleSet Sync' renamed to 'LyricStamp' across all visible surfaces (UI wordmark, window/page title, Electron dialogs+logs, package productName, README/NOTICE).
criteria_results.notes: No formal contract (rebrand, not a contracted issue). Verified via packaged-app e2e: .wordmark .name toBeVisible + toContainText('LyricStamp') on the real signed LyricStamp.app; build produced LyricStamp.app + LyricStamp-0.1.1 dmg/zip.

criteria_results.text: Renaming package name/productName/appId must NOT move the session store or break installed remote scripts (userData pin, session-store appName, ableset-sync localStorage/IDB, ableset-2 handshake all preserved).
criteria_results.notes: Runtime-proven: launched the signed LyricStamp.app (appId=com.lyricstamp); GET /api/sessions returned all 5 real sessions matching the 5 on-disk .json files under the original ~/Library/Application Support/ableset-lyrics-sync/sessions-data/. electron/main.ts pins userData to <appData>/ableset-lyrics-sync before whenReady.

process_issues.description: A rename of a user-facing string left one stale assertion in the packaged-app e2e (verification.spec.ts asserted the wordmark contained 'Sync'). It was NOT findable by the brand grep sweeps because the assertion used the bare generic substring 'Sync', not the full 'AbleSet Sync' brand phrase. Only the packaged-app e2e (Required Evidence #9) surfaced it. Non-P (does not match P1-P4); descriptive label keeps it out of the phantom-P1 trend.
process_issues.detected_by: npm run test:e2e:app — 1 failed / 46 passed; expected substring 'Sync', received 'LyricStamp'

observed_chain: in-process coding (display-only rename + userData pin; tightly-scoped, Guard 5 in-process)
verification-gate (Skill; typecheck/lint/133 unit/build + electron:dist signed LyricStamp.app + 47 packaged-app e2e; caught + fixed stale 'Sync' assertion, re-ran green)

issues.description: No skill in the chain expanded its Completion Checklist into TodoWrite/Task items at start, despite each SKILL.md requiring it. The host exposed Task tools only as deferred (TaskCreate/TaskUpdate, not a loaded TodoWrite); the orchestrator chose to proceed without loading/expanding them. Chain steps were still entered via the Skill tool with announce strings and produced their artifacts, so the discipline held in substance but not in the checklist form.
issues.detected_by: transcript review — no TodoWrite/TaskCreate call precedes any skill's work this run

workflow_adherence.notes: Substantively clean run: every chain step entered via Skill tool, announced, and produced its artifact; the single repair (e2e assertion) was caught by verification-gate's packaged-runtime parity check (#9), not a downstream smoke — the gate working as designed. Scored 'partial' only for the W3 (checklist-form not expanded). No planning-agent/acceptance-contract (not a contracted feature). Repo rename done as orchestrator infra; local dir rename still pending. Doc scope visible-surface-only by user choice.

suggested_follow_up: For brand/string renames, add a verification step that greps the test suite for the bare display substring (not just the full brand phrase) — or rely on packaged-app e2e (#9) as the catch, which worked here.
